result:
Refactored the app so `main.go` is now mostly orchestration and delegation, with extracted domain code in [pkg/notes/notes.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/pkg/notes/notes.go), [tasks/tasks.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/tasks/tasks.go), [web/web.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/web/web.go), and [sync/sync.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/sync/sync.go). The compatibility layer in [refactor_adapters.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/refactor_adapters.go) preserves the existing `main` package entry points and test-facing helpers while routing note parsing/loading, task parsing/toggling, markdown rendering/filter parsing, and git sync through the new packages.

I also added targeted package tests in [pkg/notes/notes_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/pkg/notes/notes_test.go), [tasks/tasks_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/tasks/tasks_test.go), and [web/web_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/web/web_test.go) to lock down key extracted behavior.

I could not run `go test`: the environment does not have a Go toolchain installed, so verification is limited to static review of the edits.
